concerns.description: Link injection issues
concerns.analysis: Checked the output for missing hyperlinks, specifically within table cells. In the source PDF, the '5 Species Listings' table on page 38 contains several hyperlinked text entries (e.g., '(Danaus plexippus)', '(Lasmigona subviridis)', 'James Spinymussel', and 'Implementation Progress'). In the output JSON, these text segments are extracted as plain text within the table cells, losing their inline hyperlinked status.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Table on page 38 contains clickable hyperlinks such as '(Danaus plexippus)' and 'James Spinymussel'.
concerns.output_quote: The text appears as plain text inside the table cell: 'monarch butterfly\n(Danaus plexippus)'.

concerns.description: Images missing or broken
concerns.analysis: Several meaningful diagrams and maps from the source PDF are completely missing from the rendered HTML. For example, the full-page diagrams for the Soil Contaminant Concentration Map (page 18), Groundwater Contaminant Concentration Map (page 19), Sub-Slab Gas Map (page 20), and the Survey Plat (page 61) are absent. Only their text annotations and tables were extracted.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Full-page diagram titled "SOIL CONTAMINANT CONCENTRATION MAP" showing the site layout, contours, and sample locations.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML contains the title "SOIL CONTAMINANT CONCENTRATION MAP" and extracted data tables/notes, but no element corresponding to the map graphic itself.

concerns.description: Missing images: 38 of 64 source image(s) not present in output
concerns.analysis: image_count_source=64, html_img_count=26, image_count_ratio=1.296875. Over half of source images are missing from the output.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly
concerns.analysis: On page 28, there is a numbered list of questions (1 through 4) with paragraph answers below each question. In the JSON output, this single continuous numbered list is split into four separate `ordered` list objects, with the paragraph answers situated between them. In HTML, each separate `<ol>` starts counting from 1. This flattens the continuous numbering (1., 2., 3., 4.) into four separate lists that all start with 1.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
1. Are there navigable water bodies or tributaries to a navigable water body on or within a one-half mile radius of the site?
Yes, two unnamed tributaries...
2. Are there any water bodies anywhere on or within one-half mile of the site?

concerns.description: Content visually duplicated on page
concerns.analysis: At the very end of the HTML output, the header and opening lines of the letter to Ms. Angel Wyatt (from page 72) are erroneously repeated after the sign-off from page 73.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 72 begins the letter to Ms. Angel Wyatt. Page 73 concludes the letter with the sign-off from Jay W. King.
concerns.output_quote:
Ms. Angel Wyatt
Rockingham County Environmental Health Director
PO Box 204
Wentworth, NC 27375

Dear Ms. Wyatt:
concerns.location: End of document (following the content of Page 73)

concerns.description: Missing table(s): source PDF has tables not found in output on p20(source:8,output:1)
concerns.analysis: source_tables=28, output_tables=38. Pages with missing tables: p20(source:8,output:1)

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 1 table(s) with matching structure across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=1. Examples: pages 38-39: 7 cols (group, name...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements. In HTML, these should be one continuous table to maintain accessibility and reading order.

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 16 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis: caption_as_header=16. Examples: p17: "SITE MAP"; p18: "SB-10 (12/13/17)"; p18: "SB-05 (1/27/16)". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (4 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=4. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 43 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=43, examples=['https://harthick.sharepoint.com/sites/MasterFiles-1/Shared D', 'https://fws.maps.arcgis.com/home/webmap/print.html', 'https://ecos.fws.gov/ecp/']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (109): i; ii; https://harthick.sharepoint.com/sites/MasterFiles-1/Shared D
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=109, pages_affected=51/73. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: 3 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: http://www.fws.gov/birds/management/managed-species/birds-of-conservation-concer; http://www.fws.gov/birds/management/project-assessment-tools-and-guidance/conser; http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/wm/DSCA/PublicNotices.
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=3. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: List broken by page break (1 instance(s)): p53→p54: list item ends “… but are not limited to: modification or”, continues as paragraph
concerns.analysis: split_list_count=1. Lists that span page breaks should be extracted as a single continuous structure. Split lists affect reading order and navigability for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly: 1 ordered list(s) have alphabetical markers (A, B, C) rendered as numeric — e.g. "a. child care centers or schools; or"
concerns.analysis: alpha_numeric_confusion=1. Ordered list items start with sequential letter prefixes (A., B., C.) but are rendered as numeric lists, losing the alphabetical semantics.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 3 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "TITLE", "TITLE", "Dedicated Nature Preserve"; 17 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=3, no_th_tables=17. Tables use <th> for data values, have a data row mismarked as a header row, or lack <th> elements entirely. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Form elements detected: 10 form(s) with 25 field(s) — forms lose functionality in HTML
concerns.analysis: form_count=10, total_fields=25. PDF form fields (checkboxes, text inputs, signatures) cannot be faithfully rendered as static HTML.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 50 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (50 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...ic of three wavy blue and green shapes between the"; p1: "... locations: one in Charlotte, NC at 2923 South Try"; p2: "...hic of three stacked, wavy black shapes separating"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=50 (truncated=50, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

concerns.description: Table row count mismatch: p18(source:6rows,output:3rows), p61(source:4rows,output:8rows)
concerns.analysis: Matched tables have >20% row count difference (delta >=3). Pages: p18(source:6rows,output:3rows), p61(source:4rows,output:8rows)

concerns.description: Table column count mismatch: p16(source:3cols,output:4cols), p18(source:2cols,output:3cols), p18(source:3cols,output:2cols), p18(source:4cols,output:3cols), p19(source:3cols,output:2cols) (+1 more)
concerns.analysis: Matched tables have different column counts. Pages: p16(source:3cols,output:4cols), p18(source:2cols,output:3cols), p18(source:3cols,output:2cols), p18(source:4cols,output:3cols), p19(source:3cols,output:2cols) (+1 more)
concerns.location: p16(source:3cols,output:4cols), p18(source:2cols,output:3cols), p18(source:3cols,output:2cols), p18(source:4cols,output:3cols), p19(source:3cols,output:2cols) (+1 more)

concerns.description: Degenerate images detected: 2 images with extreme aspect ratio or tiny dimensions
concerns.analysis: degenerate_count=2, threshold=2

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 1 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=1. Examples: p35: "An aerial map from the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Serv". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.
